due.title: Two-sided system view
due.excerpt: Two sided system view Expression: Everything has two sides, but they should not always be judged as simply good or bad. They are parts of a complete system. Used for: Culture, P...
due.body:
Two-sided system view

Expression:
Everything has two sides, but they should not always be judged as simply good or bad. They are parts of a complete system.

Used for:
Culture, Philosophy, Society, Opinion questions

Related:
balance, systems thinking, complexity

due.title: The winner can get the resource and survive.
due.excerpt: Grammar Upgrade 1 Original: The winner can get the resource and survive. Better: The creatures that obtain enough resources are more likely to survive. Explanation: 使用 relative...
due.body:
Grammar Upgrade 1

Original:
The winner can get the resource and survive.

Better:
The creatures that obtain enough resources are more likely to survive.

Explanation:
使用 relative clause 表达更清楚的逻辑关系；用 "obtain enough resources" 替代 "get the resource"，表达更准确；用 "are more likely to" 让语气更自然。

due.title: Zhejiang travel experience
due.excerpt: Zhejiang travel experience Story: During my trip to Zhejiang, I realized that a single travel experience can be used to describe a person, a place, an unexpected event, and a pe...
due.body:
Zhejiang travel experience

Story:
During my trip to Zhejiang, I realized that a single travel experience can be used to describe a person, a place, an unexpected event, and a personal change.

Used for:
Travel, Places, People, Unexpected Experience

Related:
flexible story bank, IELTS Part 2, personal growth

due.title: System-thinking game answer
due.excerpt: System thinking game answer Response: I think a game based on an ecosystem would be more meaningful than a simple fighting game. In this kind of game, every creature has to surv...
due.body:
System-thinking game answer

Response:
I think a game based on an ecosystem would be more meaningful than a simple fighting game. In this kind of game, every creature has to survive by finding resources and adapting to the environment. Some creatures may look weak at first, but they could become successful if the conditions change. This makes the game more realistic, because real life is also shaped by balance, competition, and adaptation.

Used for:
Technology, Games, Environment, Opinion questions

Structure:
main idea - explanation - contrast - real-life connection

Related:
ecosystem, balance, adaptation

due.excerpt: Congested Pronunciation: /kənˈdʒestɪd/ Common mistake: wrong stress Practice sentence: The roads are often congested during rush hour.

due.title: Adapt
due.excerpt: Adapt Meaning: Change successfully according to a new situation. Pronunciation: /əˈdæpt/ Example: The creatures that adapt best will survive. IELTS usage: Useful for education,...
due.body:
Adapt

Meaning:
Change successfully according to a new situation.

Pronunciation:
/əˈdæpt/

Example:
The creatures that adapt best will survive.

IELTS usage:
Useful for education, technology, work, culture, and environmental questions.

Related words:
adjust, evolve, respond

due.title: Herbivore

due.body:
Herbivore

Meaning:
An animal that eats plants.

Pronunciation:
/ˈhɜːrbɪvɔːr/

Example:
The first creature is a simple herbivore that survives by eating grass.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing animals, ecosystems, biology, and the environment.

Related words:
ecosystem, plant-eating animal, food chain

due.title: Spawn
due.excerpt: Spawn Meaning: Generate something in a game world. Pronunciation: /spɔːn/ Example: Players can spawn different creatures in the ecosystem. IELTS usage: Useful for describing gam...
due.body:
Spawn

Meaning:
Generate something in a game world.

Pronunciation:
/spɔːn/

Example:
Players can spawn different creatures in the ecosystem.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing games, technology, simulations, and artificial systems.

Related words:
generate, create, appear

due.title: Thrive
due.excerpt: Thrive Meaning: Grow successfully and become strong. Pronunciation: /θraɪv/ Example: The creatures that adapt best thrive in the ecosystem. IELTS usage: Useful for describing pe...
due.body:
Thrive

Meaning:
Grow successfully and become strong.

Pronunciation:
/θraɪv/

Example:
The creatures that adapt best thrive in the ecosystem.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing people, communities, businesses, and natural systems.

Related words:
flourish, succeed, develop

upcoming.title: AI Game Ideas
upcoming.excerpt: Session Topic AI Game Development 1. Band 7.5 8.0 Model Answer I would like to design a game based on a living ecosystem. In this game, different creatures can spawn in a shared...
upcoming.body:
Session Topic

AI Game Development

1. Band 7.5-8.0 Model Answer

I would like to design a game based on a living ecosystem. In this game, different creatures can spawn in a shared environment, and each creature needs to obtain resources in order to survive. Some of them may be herbivores that depend on plants, while others may develop different survival strategies over time.

What interests me most is that the game would not simply have a fixed winner. Instead, the creatures that adapt best to the changing environment would be more likely to thrive. This makes the game feel more natural and meaningful, because it reflects how systems work in real life.

I think this kind of game could also help players understand that everything has two sides. A creature that seems weak in one environment may become powerful in another. So the goal is not just to defeat others, but to understand relationships, resources, and long-term balance.

2. New Vocabulary

Spawn

Meaning:
Generate something in a game world.

Pronunciation:
/spɔːn/

Example:
Players can spawn different creatures in the ecosystem.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing games, technology, simulations, and artificial systems.

Related words:
generate, create, appear

Herbivore

Meaning:
An animal that eats plants.

Pronunciation:
/ˈhɜːrbɪvɔːr/

Example:
The first creature is a simple herbivore that survives by eating grass.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing animals, ecosystems, biology, and the environment.

Related words:
ecosystem, plant-eating animal, food chain

Adapt

Meaning:
Change successfully according to a new situation.

Pronunciation:
/əˈdæpt/

Example:
The creatures that adapt best will survive.

IELTS usage:
Useful for education, technology, work, culture, and environmental questions.

Related words:
adjust, evolve, respond

Thrive

Meaning:
Grow successfully and become strong.

Pronunciation:
/θraɪv/

Example:
The creatures that adapt best thrive in the ecosystem.

IELTS usage:
Useful for describing people, communities, businesses, and natural systems.

Related words:
flourish, succeed, develop

3. Grammar Upgrade

Original:
The winner can get the resource and survive.

Better:
The creatures that obtain enough resources are more likely to survive.

Explanation:
使用 relative clause 表达更清楚的逻辑关系；用 "obtain enough resources" 替代 "get the resource"，表达更准确；用 "are more likely to" 让语气更自然。

4. Pronunciation Notes

Congested

Pronunciation:
/kənˈdʒestɪd/

Common mistake:
wrong stress

Practice sentence:
The roads are often congested during rush hour.

5. Personal Expression Bank

Two-sided system view

Expression:
Everything has two sides, but they should not always be judged as simply good or bad. They are parts of a complete system.

Used for:
Culture, Philosophy, Society, Opinion questions

Related:
balance, systems thinking, complexity

6. Mini Speaking Responses

System-thinking game answer

Response:
I think a game based on an ecosystem would be more meaningful than a simple fighting game. In this kind of game, every creature has to survive by finding resources and adapting to the environment. Some creatures may look weak at first, but they could become successful if the conditions change. This makes the game more realistic, because real life is also shaped by balance, competition, and adaptation.

Used for:
Technology, Games, Environment, Opinion questions

Structure:
main idea - explanation - contrast - real-life connection

Related:
ecosystem, balance, adaptation

7. Personal Stories

Zhejiang travel experience

Story:
During my trip to Zhejiang, I realized that a single travel experience can be used to describe a person, a place, an unexpected event, and a personal change.

Used for:
Travel, Places, People, Unexpected Experience

Related:
flexible story bank, IELTS Part 2, personal growth

8. Topic Links

- Technology
- Games
- Environment
- Society

9. Skill Links

- Explain Reasons
- Describe Systems
- Express Personal Opinions
